name: SS-31
subtitle: Mitochondrial-Targeted Peptide | Cardiolipin Protector

modifications: Selectively binds cardiolipin in inner mitochondrial membrane

overview: SS-31 (Elamipretide) is an aromatic-cationic tetrapeptide that selectively binds to cardiolipin in the inner mitochondrial membrane, preventing lipid peroxidation and optimizing electron transport chain function for enhanced cellular energy production.
mechanism: Selectively binds to cardiolipin in inner mitochondrial membrane, preventing lipid peroxidation and optimizing electron transport chain function. This improves ATP production and reduces oxidative damage at the mitochondrial level.
keyBenefits: Excellent safety profile in clinical trials

items.name: Enhanced ATP Production
items.description: Optimizes electron transport chain function for improved cellular energy.

items.name: Oxidative Stress Reduction
items.description: Prevents cardiolipin peroxidation and reduces mitochondrial oxidative damage.

items.name: Mitochondrial Disease Support
items.description: Significant improvements in 6-minute walk test and fatigue scores in PMM patients.

items.name: Cardiac Protection
items.description: Reduces cardiac injury markers and improves mitochondrial function in surgical patients.

items.name: Heart Failure Support
items.description: Reduces ischemia-reperfusion injury and supports cardiac function.

items.name: Exercise Tolerance
items.description: Enhanced exercise capacity and reduced fatigue.

items.name: Age-Related Muscle Function
items.description: Reversed age-related decline in muscle function in animal studies.

items.name: Cognitive Protection
items.description: Neuroprotective effects demonstrated in Alzheimer's models.

items.name: Cellular Rejuvenation
items.description: Supports overall cellular health through mitochondrial optimization.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous or intravenous injection. SS-31 dissolves readily and should be protected from light due to photosensitivity.

steps: Optional: sterile filtration for IV use
Protect from light - use amber vials if available

deliveryMethods.overview: Topical cream/gel formulation available with limited research data. Used primarily for anti-aging skin applications.

interactions.notes: Both support mitochondrial function through different mechanisms.
Both are mitochondrial peptides with complementary mechanisms.
May work together to protect cellular function.
BPC-157 focuses on tissue repair while SS-31 targets cellular energetics.

timeline.effects: Improved stamina, clearer thinking, better sleep

common: Excellent safety profile in clinical trials
No significant side effects reported at therapeutic doses
Safe for long-term use based on current data

good: Pharmaceutical grade (>98% purity required for clinical applications)
warning: Light sensitive - protect from exposure using amber vials
bad: Yellow or brown discoloration indicates degradation

references.title: Randomized Dose-Escalation Trial of Elamipretide in Adults with Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy (MMPOWER)
references.keyFindings: Phase I/II trial in 36 patients showed dose-dependent improvement in 6-minute walk test (64.5m vs 20.4m placebo at highest dose) after 5 days of treatment with excellent safety profile.

references.title: Novel Mitochondria-Targeting Peptide in Heart Failure Treatment (EMBRACE)
references.keyFindings: IV infusion in heart failure patients showed significant decrease in left ventricular end-diastolic volume (-18 mL, P=0.009) and end-systolic volume (-14 mL, P=0.005) at the highest dose.

references.title: Improving Mitochondrial Function with SS-31 Reverses Age-Related Redox Stress and Improves Exercise Tolerance in Aged Mice
references.keyFindings: 8 weeks of SS-31 (3 mg/kg/day) in aged mice reversed age-related decline in mitochondrial ATP production, restored redox homeostasis, and significantly increased treadmill endurance.

references.title: TAZPOWER: Phase 2/3 Randomized Clinical Trial of Elamipretide in Barth Syndrome
references.keyFindings: 40mg daily in Barth syndrome patients. Primary endpoints not met at 12 weeks, but open-label extension showed cumulative 96.1m improvement in 6MWT at 168 weeks (P=0.003) with sustained fatigue improvements.

references.title: Efficacy and Safety of Elamipretide in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy: The MMPOWER-3 Randomized Clinical Trial
references.keyFindings: Phase 3 trial (218 patients) did not meet primary 6MWT endpoint overall, but post-hoc analysis showed improvement in patients with nuclear DNA variants. Well-tolerated with mild-to-moderate adverse events.

references.title: Elamipretide: A Review of Its Structure, Mechanism of Action, and Therapeutic Potential
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review covering PROGRESS-HF, TAZPOWER, MMPOWER-3, and ReCLAIM trials. Highlights elamipretide's unique cardiolipin-binding mechanism and broad therapeutic potential across mitochondrial diseases.

latestResearch.title: FDA Accelerated Approval for Barth Syndrome
latestResearch.summary: Elamipretide (Forzinity) received FDA accelerated approval as the first cardiolipin-directed mitochondrial therapeutic for Barth syndrome in patients weighing ≥30 kg.

latestResearch.title: Long-Term TAZPOWER Extension Results (168 Weeks)
latestResearch.summary: 168-week open-label extension data showed sustained improvements in 6MWT (96.1m cumulative, P=0.003), fatigue scores, and 3D left ventricular volumes in Barth syndrome patients.

latestResearch.title: ReCLAIM-2 Phase II Trial in Age-Related Macular Degeneration
latestResearch.summary: Phase II trial evaluated elamipretide for geographic atrophy growth, visual function, and ellipsoid zone preservation in AMD patients.

latestResearch.title: Elamipretide Improves Cardiac and Skeletal Muscle Function During Aging
latestResearch.summary: Demonstrated that elamipretide improves cardiac and skeletal muscle function during aging without detectable changes in tissue epigenetic or transcriptomic age.

quickStats.frequency: Once daily (morning or pre-workout for athletic use)
quickStats.storage: Reconstituted: 2-8°C in amber/dark vials, use within 30 days. Light sensitive
